# UC-23.7.2 · First Contact Resolution Rate and Escalation Patterns

## Description

Measures **first-contact resolution (FCR)** on closed ServiceNow incidents by counting tickets that resolved without **`reassignment_count`**, without **`reopen_count`** churn, and without journal language that marks an explicit escalation—then rolls up **FCR rate**, average **handle-time minutes**, and paired **escalation pressure** by **`category`** and **`assignment_group`** so support leadership sees whether routing or coaching is failing (including top escalation-heavy tuples rather than only a vanity headline percentage).

## Value

FCR is the operational KPI that ties agent readiness and queue design to labor minutes: shaving transfers from thirty percent toward twenty removes tangible average-handle-time minutes per ticket, trims repeat contacts that drag **CES** surveys (**UC-23.7.3**), and answers the support director’s question—**where is the training hole versus the routing mismatch?**—without waiting for anecdotal escalations. Unlike sibling **UC-23.7.1**, which judges **contractual SLA attainment** via **`business_duration`** and **`task_sla`**, this lens stays squarely on **first-owner success**, which is how centers justify staffing models and curriculum budgets.

## Implementation

(1) Deploy **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow** (1928) with a Table input on **`incident`** into **`itsm`** (or **`business`**) using the same **`sys_updated_on`** checkpoint pattern as **UC-23.7.1**. (2) Publish the primary FCR rollup as a weekly Monday report and the daily escalation companion (see **detailedImplementation**) for trend spike response. (3) Wire executive and category-owner alerts on week-over-week FCR drops and category-level floors. (4) Pair the heatmap with a third saved search that drills **`assigned_to`** when a group-level cell stays red—coaching versus policy. (5) Record the integration user’s ServiceNow **`time_zone`** in the TA runbook so `_time` comparisons stay auditable.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• **Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow** (`Splunk_TA_snow`, Splunkbase 1928) on a Heavy Forwarder with **HTTPS/443** egress to `https://<instance>.service-now.com`, OAuth 2.0 credentials for an integration user, and matching TA fragments on Search Heads so **`snow:incident`** field extraction is consistent.
• ServiceNow RBAC: read **`incident`**, visibility into **`sys_user`** (for **`time_zone`** documentation), and optional read of **`sys_audit`** if you later reconcile reassignment deltas (not in the base SPL here).
• Splunk RBAC: analyst role grants **`srchIndexesAllowed`** for the index you choose (`itsm` in the examples—retarget consistently).
• Relationship: implement **UC-23.7.1** first when leadership also needs **SLA breach** and **`task_sla`** truth; this FCR UC reuses the same TA feed but never substitutes **FCR** for **contract compliance**.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
In **Apps → Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow → Configuration**, add the **Account** (instance URL + OAuth client). Add **Data Input → Type `Table`**: **Table Name** `incident`, **Index** `itsm`, **Source Type** `snow:incident`, **Polling Interval** `300` seconds, **Fields** enumerated so poll projections never omit FCR-critical columns: `number,sys_id,short_description,category,subcategory,assignment_group,assigned_to,state,incident_state,priority,urgency,impact,opened_at,resolved_at,closed_at,reassignment_count,work_notes,comments,reopen_count,parent_incident,caller_id,company,cmdb_ci,sys_updated_on,business_duration,calendar_duration`. When the input supports **`sysparm_query`**, bound each poll with `sys_updated_on>=javascript:gs.minutesAgoStart(15)` so you retrieve recently touched rows plus a fifteen-minute skew buffer without full-table scans.

The add-on emits ServiceNow **`state`** values as the platform returns them—often **6=Resolved** and **7=Closed** numerically, sometimes translated to **"Resolved"/"Closed"** strings after parsing—so downstream searches must tolerate both representations (the primary SPL uses **`tonumber()`** plus label fallbacks). **`_time`** on each event follows the configured **time_field** (**`sys_updated_on`** here), meaning bucketing by poll time differs from “day of closure” unless you overwrite **`_time`** with **`resolved_at`** for closure-trend panels (done in the companion search). Document which ServiceNow **`time_zone`** the integration user's profile uses: the TA still serializes timestamps in the table’s GMT-friendly format, but analysts comparing to a ServiceNow list view must match zones or exports in UTC.

On the ServiceNow side, validate that **`reassignment_count`** increments for real dispatcher workflow (not only scripted **`assign()`** shortcuts that bypass counters—see Step 5). Confirm **`reopen_count`** increments for the reopen paths your CAB actually uses. Plan **365+** days of index retention if leadership reviews YoY seasonality.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**
Primary rollup (schedule **Monday 06:00** in the support org’s timezone with time range **last 7 days @d** for standups; clone to **last 30 days** for monthly leadership packs):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d@d
| eval _time=strptime(sys_updated_on, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| dedup sys_id sortby -_time
| eval state_raw=coalesce(state, incident_state)
| eval state_num=tonumber(state_raw)
| eval state_l=lower(trim(tostring(state_raw)))
| eval resolved_ok=if(isnotnull(state_num), if(state_num IN (6,7),1,0), if(state_l IN ("resolved","closed"),1,0))
| eval reassignment_n=coalesce(tonumber(reassignment_count),0)
| eval reopen_n=coalesce(tonumber(reopen_count), tonumber(incident_reopened_count), tonumber(u_incident_reopened_count), 0)
| eval wn=coalesce(mvjoin(work_notes," "),work_notes,"")
| eval cm=coalesce(mvjoin(comments," "),comments,"")
| eval notes_for_match=lower(trim(wn." ".cm))
| eval fp_noise=if(match(notes_for_match,"(?i)(no\\s+escalation|not\\s+(an\\s+)?escalation|de[\\s-]?escalat)"),1,0)
| eval tp_esc=if(match(notes_for_match,"(?i)(escalat(ed|ing\\s+to|ion\\s+to)|tier[\\s-]?[23]|moved\\s+to\\s+(l[23]|tier\\s*[23]))"),1,0)
| eval phrase_escalation=if(fp_noise==1,0,tp_esc)
| eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval resolved_epoch=strptime(coalesce(resolved_at, closed_at), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval handle_time_minutes=if(isnotnull(opened_epoch) AND isnotnull(resolved_epoch), round((resolved_epoch-opened_epoch)/60,1), null())
| eval fcr=if(resolved_ok==1 AND reassignment_n==0 AND reopen_n==0 AND phrase_escalation==0,1,0)
| eval escalated=if(reassignment_n>=2 OR phrase_escalation==1 OR reopen_n>0,1,0)
| where resolved_ok=1
| stats count as resolved, sum(fcr) as first_contact, avg(handle_time_minutes) as avg_aht by category, assignment_group
| eval fcr_rate=round(100*first_contact/resolved,1)
| sort - resolved
| head 50
```

The window **`earliest=-30d@d`** mirrors executive reporting cadence; tighten to seven days for operational steering. **`dedup sys_id`** collapses duplicate polls into the newest lifecycle snapshot keyed off **`sys_updated_on`**. **`resolved_ok`** encodes ServiceNow closure semantics without trusting English-only filters. **`fcr`** joins three operational signals: zero **`reassignment_count`** (after **`coalesce`**), zero **`reopen_count`**, and no affirmative escalation phrase once boilerplate negatives strip **`fp_noise`**. **`escalated`** highlights churn (**`reassignment_n>=2`**, narrative escalation, or reopen) for sister KPI panels without flipping **`fcr`** when a shop legitimately records exactly one routing hop.

Priority-aware companion—clone the middle section and change the closing **`stats`** to **`by category, assignment_group, priority_label`** after you add **`priority_label`** with a **`case()`** on **`priority`**—so P1–P2 FCR is not masked by steady P4 work.

Escalation trend (schedule **daily**, use **closure time** bucketing):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d@d
| eval _time=strptime(sys_updated_on, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| dedup sys_id sortby -_time
| eval close_t=strptime(coalesce(resolved_at, closed_at), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval _time=close_t
| eval state_raw=coalesce(state, incident_state)
| eval state_num=tonumber(state_raw)
| eval state_l=lower(trim(tostring(state_raw)))
| eval resolved_ok=if(isnotnull(state_num), if(state_num IN (6,7),1,0), if(state_l IN ("resolved","closed"),1,0))
| eval reassignment_n=coalesce(tonumber(reassignment_count),0)
| eval reopen_n=coalesce(tonumber(reopen_count), tonumber(incident_reopened_count), tonumber(u_incident_reopened_count), 0)
| eval wn=coalesce(mvjoin(work_notes," "),work_notes,"")
| eval cm=coalesce(mvjoin(comments," "),comments,"")
| eval notes_for_match=lower(trim(wn." ".cm))
| eval fp_noise=if(match(notes_for_match,"(?i)(no\\s+escalation|not\\s+(an\\s+)?escalation|de[\\s-]?escalat)"),1,0)
| eval tp_esc=if(match(notes_for_match,"(?i)(escalat(ed|ing\\s+to|ion\\s+to)|tier[\\s-]?[23]|moved\\s+to\\s+(l[23]|tier\\s*[23]))"),1,0)
| eval phrase_escalation=if(fp_noise==1,0,tp_esc)
| eval escalated=if(reassignment_n>=2 OR phrase_escalation==1 OR reopen_n>0,1,0)
| where resolved_ok=1 AND isnotnull(_time)
| timechart span=1d sum(escalated) as escalations count as total
| eval escalation_pct=if(total>0, round(100*escalations/total,1), null())
```

Alerts: fleet-wide **`fcr_rate`** drop **>5** percentage points week-over-week emails **support-ops leadership**; **`category`** **`fcr_rate`** below **50%** pages the category owner; throttle by **`assignment_group`** to avoid duplicate nights.

**Step 3 — Validate**
If the customer licenses **Performance Analytics**, open ServiceNow’s native FCR or reassignment widgets for the same window—variance usually traces to definition drift (category change counted as reassignment in PA but not here) or **`state`** filters, not ingestion failure. Pull **five** high-FCR and **five** low-FCR **`category`–`assignment_group`** pairs from Splunk; for **three** incidents in each bucket, verify **`number`** in the UI against **`assignment_group`**, **`reassignment_count`**, **`reopen_count`**, and **`work_notes`** narrative. When Splunk shows **`reassignment_count=0`** but the UI timeline shows multiple assignments, capture whether the reassignment landed after the last poll—advance **`sys_updated_on`** on the record and confirm the next poll reflects the counters. For regex quality, sample **fifty** **`work_notes`** hits with **`notes_for_match`** containing escalation tokens and tally false positives (“no escalation needed”)—if noise exceeds a few percent, tighten **`tp_esc`** further.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
Dashboard ordering: headline **`splunk.singlevalue`** FCR, secondary tiles by **`priority_label`**, **`splunk.heatmap`** on **`category`** × **`assignment_group`**, sortable drill table by escalation pressure, embedded **`splunk.timechart`** escalation companion with **`span=1d`**. Weekly cadence covers coaching narratives; daily escalation charts catch spikes tied to releases or outages without waiting seven days. When a heatmap cell stays cold only for one **`assignment_group`** while sibling cells are warm inside the same **`category`**, escalate routing experiments (skills-based routing tests). When every group struggles inside the same **`category`**, prioritize curriculum or vendor defects instead of queue tweaks. Assign executive accountability to the **support operations director** and require **`assigned_to`**-level drill searches before HR interventions.

**Step 5 — Troubleshoot**
• **`reassignment_count` silently NULL on legacy or imported incidents** — Older migrated tickets sometimes ship without counters populated; **`coalesce(...,0)`** treats NULL as pristine FCR and inflates scores. Run `index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-90d | stats count(eval(isnull(reassignment_count))) as null_rows count as total | eval null_pct=round(100*null_rows/total,1)` and, if **`null_pct>1`**, constrain reporting to `isnotnull(reassignment_count)` or segment by **`sys_created_on`** quarters. Mitigation path: ingest **`sys_audit`** with a separate input and count group/assignee transitions for those legacy sys_ids (heavier compute, audit-grade).

• **Work-notes regex over-match** — Broad patterns still catch benign template language. Pull `index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-7d work_notes=*escalat* | head 50 | table number work_notes`, read manually, then iterate the **`fp_noise`**/**`tp_esc`** split; optional long-term fix is configuring the TA to ingest **`sys_journal_field`** rows as **`snow:incident_work_notes`** events for entry-level matching instead of concatenated blobs.

• **Reopens masquerading as FCR successes** — Tickets that close then return to **In Progress** days later—or new incidents for the same root cause—invalidate “first touch fixed it” stories. Detect `index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-60d | stats values(state) as journey min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen by number | eval bounce=mvcount(journey)` style checks, compare to **`reopen_count`**, and extend the FCR denominator with **`caller_id`** duplicate heuristics when reopen teams file fresh records instead of reopening originals. Document the **30-day** reopen policy you choose so finance and CX analytics stay aligned.


## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d@d
| eval _time=strptime(sys_updated_on, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| dedup sys_id sortby -_time
| eval state_raw=coalesce(state, incident_state)
| eval state_num=tonumber(state_raw)
| eval state_l=lower(trim(tostring(state_raw)))
| eval resolved_ok=if(isnotnull(state_num), if(state_num IN (6,7),1,0), if(state_l IN ("resolved","closed"),1,0))
| eval reassignment_n=coalesce(tonumber(reassignment_count),0)
| eval reopen_n=coalesce(tonumber(reopen_count), tonumber(incident_reopened_count), tonumber(u_incident_reopened_count), 0)
| eval wn=coalesce(mvjoin(work_notes," "),work_notes,"")
| eval cm=coalesce(mvjoin(comments," "),comments,"")
| eval notes_for_match=lower(trim(wn." ".cm))
| eval fp_noise=if(match(notes_for_match,"(?i)(no\\s+escalation|not\\s+(an\\s+)?escalation|de[\\s-]?escalat)"),1,0)
| eval tp_esc=if(match(notes_for_match,"(?i)(escalat(ed|ing\\s+to|ion\\s+to)|tier[\\s-]?[23]|moved\\s+to\\s+(l[23]|tier\\s*[23]))"),1,0)
| eval phrase_escalation=if(fp_noise==1,0,tp_esc)
| eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval resolved_epoch=strptime(coalesce(resolved_at, closed_at), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval handle_time_minutes=if(isnotnull(opened_epoch) AND isnotnull(resolved_epoch), round((resolved_epoch-opened_epoch)/60,1), null())
| eval fcr=if(resolved_ok==1 AND reassignment_n==0 AND reopen_n==0 AND phrase_escalation==0,1,0)
| eval escalated=if(reassignment_n>=2 OR phrase_escalation==1 OR reopen_n>0,1,0)
| where resolved_ok=1
| stats count as resolved, sum(fcr) as first_contact, avg(handle_time_minutes) as avg_aht by category, assignment_group
| eval fcr_rate=round(100*first_contact/resolved,1)
| sort - resolved
| head 50
```

## Visualization

(1) **`splunk.singlevalue`** for rolling fleet FCR with a seven-day sparkline. (2) Row of three tiles splitting FCR by **`priority_label`** bands (P1–P2 vs P3–P4 vs P5/Unk) because severities legitimately reassign. (3) **`splunk.heatmap`** with **`category`** × **`assignment_group`** cell text **`fcr_rate`**. (4) Companion **`splunk.timechart`** from the escalation search (daily **`escalation_pct`**). (5) **`splunk.table`** of **`category`**, **`assignment_group`**, **`resolved`**, **`fcr_rate`**, **`avg_aht`**, sortable by escalation rate via a mirrored search. (6) Drilldown on **`number`** to the incident form using your instance URL template.
